root_text.an4.87:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sekho hoti pāṭipado;
root_text.an4.87:2.4: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, rañño khattiyassa muddhāvasittassa jeṭṭho putto ābhiseko anabhisitto macalappatto;
root_text.an4.87:2.5: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sekho hoti pāṭipado, anuttaraṁ yogakkhemaṁ patthayamāno viharati.
root_text.an4.87:2.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo samaṇamacalo hoti.
root_text.an4.87:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati, no ca kho aṭṭha vimokkhe kāyena phusitvā viharati.
root_text.an4.87:3.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo samaṇapuṇḍarīko hoti.
root_text.an4.87:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati, aṭṭha ca vimokkhe kāyena phusitvā viharati.
root_text.an4.87:4.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo samaṇapadumo hoti.
root_text.an4.87:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo samaṇesu samaṇasukhumālo hoti?
root_text.an4.87:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yācitova bahulaṁ cīvaraṁ paribhuñjati, appaṁ ayācito; yācitova bahulaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ paribhuñjati, appaṁ ayācito; yācitova bahulaṁ senāsanaṁ paribhuñjati, appaṁ ayācito; yācitova bahulaṁ gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhāraṁ paribhuñjati, appaṁ ayācito.
root_text.an4.87:5.3: Yehi kho pana sabrahmacārīhi saddhiṁ viharati, tyassa manāpeneva bahulaṁ kāyakammena samudācaranti, appaṁ amanāpena; manāpeneva bahulaṁ vacīkammena samudācaranti, appaṁ amanāpena; manāpeneva bahulaṁ manokammena samudācaranti, appaṁ amanāpena;
root_text.an4.87:5.4: manāpaṁyeva bahulaṁ upahāraṁ upaharanti, appaṁ amanāpaṁ.
root_text.an4.87:5.5: Yāni kho pana tāni vedayitāni pittasamuṭṭhānāni vā semhasamuṭṭhānāni vā vātasamuṭṭhānāni vā sannipātikāni vā utupariṇāmajāni vā visamaparihārajāni vā opakkamikāni vā kammavipākajāni vā, tāni panassa na bahudeva uppajjanti. Appābādho hoti.
root_text.an4.87:5.6: Catunnaṁ jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī,
root_text.an4.87:5.7: āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an4.87:5.8: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo samaṇesu samaṇasukhumālo hoti.
root_text.an4.87:6.1: Yañhi taṁ, bhikkhave, sammā vadamāno vadeyya samaṇesu samaṇasukhumāloti, mameva taṁ, bhikkhave, sammā vadamāno vadeyya samaṇesu samaṇasukhumāloti.
root_text.an4.87:6.2: Ahañhi, bhikkhave, yācitova bahulaṁ cīvaraṁ paribhuñjāmi, appaṁ ayācito; yācitova bahulaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ paribhuñjāmi, appaṁ ayācito; yācitova bahulaṁ senāsanaṁ paribhuñjāmi, appaṁ ayācito; yācitova bahulaṁ gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhāraṁ paribhuñjāmi, appaṁ ayācito.
root_text.an4.87:6.3: Yehi kho pana bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ viharāmi te me manāpeneva bahulaṁ kāyakammena samudācaranti, appaṁ amanāpena; manāpeneva bahulaṁ vacīkammena samudācaranti, appaṁ amanāpena; manāpeneva bahulaṁ manokammena samudācaranti, appaṁ amanāpena; manāpaṁyeva bahulaṁ upahāraṁ upaharanti, appaṁ amanāpaṁ.
root_text.an4.87:6.4: Yāni kho pana tāni vedayitāni pittasamuṭṭhānāni vā semhasamuṭṭhānāni vā vātasamuṭṭhānāni vā sannipātikāni vā utupariṇāmajāni vā visamaparihārajāni vā opakkamikāni vā kammavipākajāni vā, tāni me na bahudeva uppajjanti. Appābādhohamasmi.
root_text.an4.87:6.5: Catunnaṁ kho panasmi jhānānaṁ ābhicetasikānaṁ diṭṭhadhammasukhavihārānaṁ nikāmalābhī akicchalābhī akasiralābhī,
root_text.an4.87:6.6: āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharāmi.
root_text.an4.87:7.1: Yañhi taṁ, bhikkhave, sammā vadamāno vadeyya samaṇesu samaṇasukhumāloti, mameva taṁ, bhikkhave, sammā vadamāno vadeyya samaṇesu samaṇasukhumāloti.
root_text.an4.87:7.2: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.87:1.1: “Mendicants, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.87:1.3: The confirmed ascetic, the white lotus ascetic, the pink lotus ascetic, and the delicate ascetic of ascetics.
translation_text.an4.87:2.1: And how is an individual a confirmed ascetic?
translation_text.an4.87:2.2: It’s when a mendicant is a practicing trainee,
translation_text.an4.87:2.3: who lives aspiring to the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.an4.87:2.4: It’s like the eldest son of an anointed aristocratic king. He has not yet been anointed, but is eligible, and has been confirmed in the succession.
translation_text.an4.87:2.5: In the same way, a mendicant is a practicing trainee, who lives aspiring to the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.an4.87:2.6: That’s how an individual is a confirmed ascetic.
translation_text.an4.87:3.1: And how is an individual a white lotus ascetic?
translation_text.an4.87:3.2: It’s when a mendicant realizes the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And they live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements. But they don’t have direct meditative experience of the eight liberations.
translation_text.an4.87:3.3: That’s how an individual is a white lotus ascetic.
translation_text.an4.87:4.1: And how is an individual a pink lotus ascetic?
translation_text.an4.87:4.2: It’s when a mendicant realizes the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. … And they have direct meditative experience of the eight liberations.
translation_text.an4.87:4.3: That’s how an individual is a pink lotus ascetic.
translation_text.an4.87:5.1: And how is an individual a delicate ascetic of ascetics?
translation_text.an4.87:5.2: It’s when a mendicant usually uses only what they’ve been invited to accept—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick—rarely using them without invitation.
translation_text.an4.87:5.3: When living with other spiritual practitioners, they usually find themselves treated agreeably by them by way of body, speech, and mind, and rarely disagreeably.
translation_text.an4.87:5.4: And they are usually presented with agreeable things by them, rarely with disagreeable ones.
translation_text.an4.87:5.5: They’re healthy, so the various unpleasant feelings—stemming from disorders of bile, phlegm, wind, or their conjunction; or caused by change in weather, by not taking care of yourself, by overexertion, or as the result of past deeds—usually don’t come up.
translation_text.an4.87:5.6: They get the four absorptions—blissful meditations in this life that belong to the higher mind—when they want, without trouble or difficulty.
translation_text.an4.87:5.7: And they realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. …
translation_text.an4.87:5.8: That’s how an individual is a delicate ascetic of ascetics.
translation_text.an4.87:6.1: And if anyone should be rightly called a delicate ascetic of ascetics, it’s me.
translation_text.an4.87:6.2: For I usually use only what I’ve been invited to accept …
translation_text.an4.87:6.3: When living with other mendicants, I am usually treated agreeably by them.  …
translation_text.an4.87:6.5: I get the four absorptions when I want, without trouble or difficulty.
translation_text.an4.87:6.6: And I’ve realized the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. …
translation_text.an4.87:7.1: So if anyone should be rightly called a delicate ascetic of ascetics, it’s me.
translation_text.an4.87:7.2: These are the four individuals found in the world.”
